channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared in Syria in 2012, believed to be alive: Biden
item.description: “We think we can get him back," Biden told reporters at the White House, while acknowledging that “we have no direct evidence” of his status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump 'can't guarantee' tariffs won't raise prices for consumers
item.description: "I can't guarantee anything. I can't guarantee tomorrow. But I can say that if you look at my — just pre-COVID, we had the greatest economy in the history of our country," Trump responded.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio bill would require high-demand liquor bottles to be opened when sold
item.description: The bill aims to combat "flippers" who quickly buy up the limited supply of highly sought-after spirits in Ohio to illegally resell them on a secondary market for significantly more than what they paid.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, Fox 5

item.title: Why work-life balance isn't working for women
item.description: About half of working women reported feeling stressed “a lot of the day," compared to about 4 in 10 men, according to a Gallup report published this week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Stolen ruby slippers worn by Judy Garland in 'The Wizard of Oz' are auctioned for $28 million
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A pair of iconic ruby slippers that were worn by Judy Garland in “The Wizard of Oz” and stolen from a museum nearly two decades ago sold for a winning bid of $28 million at auction Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI and HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: President of Tyler Perry Studios killed in Florida plane crash
item.description: Reports say that Steve Mensch was piloting the plane that crashed on a road in Homosassa, Florida. He was the lone passenger.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Five California Lottery players become millionaires from Scratcher games
item.description: Five California Lottery players are the state’s newest millionaires after winning a combined total of $17 million from Scratcher games, lottery officials announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 5 recipes from the pros to give after-dinner cocktails some holiday flair
item.description: From a festive Old Fashioned to a boozy hot chocolate, these after-dinner drinks might inspire your own menu of holiday cocktails, to be sipped slowly by the fire or washed down with your Christmas pudding.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Miller High Life releases perfume that smells like a dive bar
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - If you've ever wondered how to describe the smell of your small local neighborhood bar, Miller High Life may have just provided that answer for you.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KRON4

item.title: Days after gunman killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, police push to ID him and FBI offers reward
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police find bag carried by gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, say he likely fled NYC on bus
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said Friday. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ohio State University fraternity suspended on hazing allegations
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) – An Ohio State University fraternity has been suspended on allegations of hazing. According to a release by a university spokesperson, the Dean of Students in the Office of Student Life has issued an interim suspension of Alpha Tau Omega based on allegations of hazing.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, NewsNation

item.title: Man on trial in the killing of an Ole Miss student gave conflicting information, police say
item.description: The man on trial in the killing of University of Mississippi student Jimmie “Jay” Lee gave conflicting information to police about how well he and Lee knew each other, according to testimony Friday by an officer who helped lead the investigation.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: MAP: Flu season is here, especially in these states
item.description: Flu activity spiked in states across the U.S. during the last week of November, according to a new report from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Appeals court upholds law ordering China-based ByteDance to sell TikTok or face U.S. ban
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday upheld a law requiring TikTok’s Chinese parent company to sell the popular app or face a U.S. ban.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KTLA 5

item.title: Johnson woos his sharpest critics in preparation for Speaker vote
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is wooing his sharpest critics in the House GOP ahead of his official election for Speaker on the House floor, working to eliminate opposition and secure the strongest leadership mandate possible in a razor-thin majority. In the latest development, Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Family awarded $310M after fatal fall on Florida ride
item.description: Tyre Sampson, 14, was tragically killed after falling to his death off a freefall tower drop ride at Orlando’s ICON Park.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, NewsNation

item.title: An Ole Miss student exchanged messages with the man now on trial in his killing, police say
item.description: In the final hours before University of Mississippi student Jimmy “Jay” Lee disappeared, sexually explicit Snapchat messages were exchanged between his account and the account of the man now on trial in his killing, an investigator testified Thursday. Sheldon “Timothy” Herrington Jr.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NASA delays Artemis moon missions to 2026, 2027
item.description: NASA is preparing to hold a news conference to discuss the agency’s Artemis program, which concerns its plans for moon launches in coming years.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fake bomb threat targets homes of insurance executive killed in NYC: Police
item.description: The threat was sent in an email to multiple people, who in turn alerted law enforcement, according to incident reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Link to adult website on Mattel's 'Wicked' dolls at center of proposed class action lawsuit
item.description: A mother claims she and her daughter visited the link on the box, only to be "horrified" by what they saw, according to the lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Tsunami warning canceled after strong California earthquake
item.description: The National Weather Service canceled a tsunami warning Thursday for a large stretch of California coastline, saying the threat had passed following a strong earthquake near Eureka.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield reverses decision to cap anesthesia coverage after a set time limit
item.description: Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield said in a statement that its decision to backpedal resulted from “significant widespread misinformation” about the policy.
item.dc:creator: Melanie Higgins, Fox 5

item.title: Concerns mount over Musk’s taste for revenge
item.description: Concerns were amplified last week after Musk went after retired Army Lt. Col. Alexander Vindman, who became an outspoken critic of President-elect Trump after testifying in his first impeachment trial.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Ammunition from UnitedHealthcare CEO shooting had words written on it: sources
item.description: Bullets found on the ground where UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson was shot Wednesday had writing on them, sources told Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Video credit: Kala Rama, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amnesty International says Israel committing genocide in Gaza. Israel rejects allegations
item.description: The offensive is among the deadliest and most destructive since World War II, and has destroyed vast areas of the besieged coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bitcoin hits $100,000 after Trump names pro-crypto SEC chair
item.description: The former president is bolstering the evolving platform amidst the threat of new currency systems seeking to challenge the U.S. dollar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, ABC 4

item.title: The Associated Press’ top photos of 2024 from the US
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — The right frame can freeze a moment in time, creating meaning for the masses from a fist pump over a bloodied ear, a bridge crumbled by a ship, towns shredded by nature, and a victory sealed with an on-field kiss. In 2024, photographers across the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge considers first lawsuit to overturn Missouri's near-total abortion ban
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Abortion-rights advocates on Wednesday asked a judge to overturn Missouri’s near-total ban on the procedure, less than a month after voters backed an abortion-rights constitutional amendment. Jackson County Circuit Judge Jerri Zhang did not immediately rule after the hearing.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sinkhole may have swallowed Pennsylvania grandmother looking for missing cat: police
item.description: Authorities fear a Pennsylvania grandmother searching for her missing cat may have been swallowed by a sinkhole.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, WGN 9
